POS ON REPLY DEC. OF STEPHANIE LAPSLEY AND AMENDED OPPOS TO DEFT'S SEP STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KYO-YA LTD., DBA THE PALACE HOTEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KYO-YA LTD.'S, DBA THE PALACE HOTEL, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE "LOCKOUT" WAS NOT PART OF A "STRIKE" WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE PARTIES AGREEMENT. THERE ALSO ARE NUMEROUS DISPUTED FACTS, PREVIOUSLY IDENTIFIED BY THE COURT, RELATED TO WHETHER IT WAS "INADVISABLE" TO USE THE HOTEL AT THE TIME. PLAINTIFF'S "AMENDED OPPOSITION" WAS NOT AUTHORIZED NOR WAS THE FILING OF THE LAPSELY DECLARATION. DECLARATIONS OF DILEO, BURGMAN AND JOHNSEN ARE ALLOWED UNDER FINKBEINER V GAVID (2006) 136 CAL. APP. 4TH 1417, 1422. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SET FORTH ON THE RECORD. MR. WEBSTER IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF KYO-YA LTD.'S, DBA THE PALACE HOTEL, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: JAMES GOLDBERG, ESQUIRE (FOLGER LEVIN & KAHN, 415.365.7362) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. BENJAMIN L. WEBSTER, ESQUIRE (PILSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, 916.329.4723 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT).
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE "LOCKOUT" WAS NOT PART OF A "STRIKE" WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE PARTIES AGREEMENT. THERE ALSO ARE NUMEROUS DISPUTED FACTS, PREVIOUSLY IDENTIFIED BY THE COURT, RELATED TO WHETHER IT WAS "INADVISABLE" TO USE THE HOTEL AT THE TIME. PLAINTIFF'S "AMENDED OPPOSITION" WAS NOT AUTHORIZED NOR WAS THE FILING OF THE LAPSELY DECLARATION. DECLARATIONS OF DILEO, BURGMAN AND JOHNSEN ARE ALLOWED UNDER FINKBEINER V GAVID (2006) 136 CAL. APP. 4TH 1417, 1422. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SET FORTH ON THE RECORD. MR. WEBSTER IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/KD) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
